mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which raises expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS changes and 20-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the positive-return hit rate following PAYEMS increases is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% embeds restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt service costs, suppressing S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a synchronized contraction in consumption and output that reduces earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and INDPRO fails to produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in >50% of coincident instances, or a regression interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation forces equity markets to price additional rate hikes, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months identified as tightening regimes, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals rising manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, which lifts broad corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,200 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances when HOUST < 1200 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in nonfarm payrolls confirm labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate household income and risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month UNRATE increases and PAYEMS decreases fail to coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a nested model.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points triggers rapid risk-off repositioning in equity markets as recession probability rises, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises >0.2pp month-over-month during walk-forward testing.
